name: Radish (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw radish root, Raphanus sativus); concentration_mM computed assuming ~100 g ≈ 100 mL

components.name: water
components.evidence: Radish root is ~95% water; solvent/base of the medium.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein; amino-acid profile below is the protein-derived N source.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Mostly cellulose/pectin; largely non-fermentable by many models but a polysaccharide reservoir.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169276; corroborated by Agronomy 2025 15(5):1216 doi:10.3390/agronomy15051216 (root glucose 1.07-1.75 g/100g)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source. Glucose is the most abundant free sugar in radish root.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169276; corroborated by Agronomy 2025 15(5):1216 (root fructose 0.38-0.73 g/100g)
components.evidence: Second free sugar; hexoses dominate over sucrose in radish root.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 169276; corroborated by Agronomy 2025 15(5):1216 (root sucrose 0.01-0.10 g/100g)
components.evidence: Minor; sucrose content is very low relative to hexoses in radish root.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Agronomy 2025 15(5):1216 doi:10.3390/agronomy15051216 (radish root oxalic 13.36-25.94 mg/100g, midpoint ~20)
components.evidence: Predominant organic acid in radish root (>90% of the organic-acid pool). Value is midpoint of the reported root range.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Detected in radish taproot but not individually quantified in root FW; present at low level (root pH 5.5-6.0 indicates weak total acidity).

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: TCA-cycle intermediate detected in radish root by GC-TOFMS; low, not individually quantified in FW.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Detected in taproot metabolome; trace, unquantified in FW.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: TCA intermediate present in radish root; trace, unquantified in FW.

components.name: malonic acid
components.source: ResearchGate 8261995 'Raphanus sativus: Their Chemistry and Biology'
components.evidence: Reported as one of the four principal radish-root organic acids (oxalic, malic, malonic, erythorbic); not quantified in FW.

components.name: L-glutamic acid
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; major organic-N and potential C source.

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.evidence: Second amino acid.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Reported as cystine (Cys dimer).

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Principal water-soluble vitamin in radish; also a redox agent.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Osmolyte / methyl donor.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine osmolyte present in radish; not quantified in USDA panel.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphate (P)
components.evidence: P source; mM computed as phosphate.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Trace mineral (Fe2+/Fe3+).

components.name: molybdate (Mo)
components.evidence: Ultra-trace essential metal (nitrate reductase, etc.); kept as trace, not quantified by USDA.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Radish root is essentially fat-free (total fat 0.10 g); fatty acids listed for completeness.

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: non-nutritive toxic heavy metal / trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant element, not a required growth nutrient

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive structural/trace element, not a microbial growth nutrient

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element (K analogue), not a required nutrient

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal-form) vitamin A; does not occur in a plant root (USDA vitamin A = 0). Plant provides only carotenoids

remove.name: Beta-Carotene
remove.reason: negligible/absent in white radish ROOT (USDA vitamin A activity = 0 RAE); carotene occurs in radish leaves/tops, not the root matrix curated here

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: FooDB compartment/model artifact, not a distinct food nutrient; iron already covered by 'iron'

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redundant redox-state duplicate of 'iron' (single Fe pool curated)

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: FooDB ionic-form artifact; cobalt is only relevant bound in B12, absent in radish root

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: atmospheric gas placeholder, not a food-matrix nutrient

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: atmospheric gas placeholder, not a food-matrix nutrient

missing_added: oxalic acid (dominant radish organic acid, quantified)
malic acid, citric acid, succinic acid, fumaric acid, malonic acid (organic acids, present/trace)
individual free sugars glucose/fructose/sucrose with real amounts (root-specific, not just total sugars)
pantothenic acid, vitamin K1, choline (were partly implicit) with amounts
keep_uncovered: boron (plant micronutrient; genuine but no BiGG metabolite / negligible for microbial growth)
notes: Raw radish root is a dilute, low-acid, sugar-dominated medium: the dominant carbon/energy sources are D-glucose (~1.05 g/100g) and D-fructose (~0.71 g/100g), with only trace sucrose. Organic-acid load is low (root pH ~5.5-6.0) and led by oxalic acid (~20 mg/100g); malic/citric/succinic/fumaric are present at trace levels. Protein and free amino acids are low, led by glutamate and aspartate. USDA free-sugar values were independently corroborated by Agronomy 2025 root-specific HPAEC data.
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169276 (Radishes, raw; SR Legacy) — sugars, amino acids, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids
Agronomy 2025, 15(5):1216 doi:10.3390/agronomy15051216 — radish root sugars & oxalic acid (root-specific)
Molecules 2016, 21(2):157 doi:10.3390/molecules21020157 — radish metabolite profiling (organic acids, TCA intermediates)
Frontiers in Nutrition 2022 doi:10.3389/fnut.2022.889407 — radish taproot organic-acid metabolomics, root pH 5.49-6.04
ResearchGate 8261995 — 'Raphanus sativus (Radish): Their Chemistry and Biology' (principal root organic acids)
FooDB FOOD00153 (Radish) — starting compound list / osmolytes
